description_long: This is a classic propeller-driven passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a distinctive triple-tail design, painted in the livery of Iberia Líneas Aéreas Españolas. The aircraft features four large radial engines mounted on a straight, low-mounted wing, each with a three-bladed propeller. The fuselage is predominantly white with a red and gold stripe running along the side, and the airline's branding is clearly visible. The tail section is notable for its three vertical stabilizers, each adorned with the Spanish flag colors. The landing gear is extended, with two main gear units under the wings and a nose wheel. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the flight crew.
description: A vintage Iberia passenger airplane with four propeller engines, a triple-tail design, and a white, red, and gold livery. The aircraft has a streamlined fuselage, low-mounted wings, and extended landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four propeller engines, low-mounted straight wings, and the nose of the airplane.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the Iberia branding, passenger windows, and the triple-tail configuration.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the three vertical stabilizers and the full wingspan.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the undercarriage, engine placement, and tail design.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral and no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Four radial piston engines, nacelle-mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Triple vertical stabilizer with conventional horizontal stabilizers, forming a distinctive triple-tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units under the wings and a single nose gear, all extended.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, typical of multi-seat passenger aircraft.
